title: Modules, Packages, Environments, and Command Lines · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: Turn the reporting pipeline into an installable and repeatable command-line tool. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in an installable data-report command and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain module search, import caching, names, qualified access, and circular-import failure conditions.
Organize public module functions, constants, docstrings, and private implementation names around stable responsibilities.
Use the main guard to separate importable behavior from command-line execution and testing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
